text.title: Implementation Plan Required Sections
text.summary: All required sections of an implementation-plan.md with purpose and structure for each.

list.item: Version, Design path, Status (Draft/In Progress/Complete), Author, Date, Project
Reference Documents table: Constitution, Spec, Design, Contracts — same pattern as design.md
Overview: 1-2 paragraphs with total tasks, token estimate, critical path complexity, wave count

rule.statement: List infrastructure, environment, code, and documentation prerequisites as explicit checkboxes before defining any tasks.
rule.rationale: Executor agents that start task execution without checking prerequisites fail mid-execution when a database migration or auth middleware does not exist. Explicit prerequisites enable pre-flight validation.

list.item: Infrastructure: database running, Redis configured, external API keys obtained
Environment: dev environment set up, env vars configured, access permissions
Code Dependencies: base service class exists, auth middleware exists, test framework configured

rule.statement: Build the dependency graph before the wave analysis table — the graph is the input to wave grouping; inverting the order produces incorrect waves.
rule.rationale: Wave analysis groups tasks by dependency level. Without a correct dependency graph, tasks end up in waves that assume non-existent outputs, producing runtime failures when the executor tries to import from a file not yet created.

list.item: FS (Finish-to-Start): TASK-002 starts when TASK-001 finishes (most common)
SS (Start-to-Start): TASK-002 can start when TASK-001 starts
FF (Finish-to-Finish): TASK-002 finishes when TASK-001 finishes
Use arrow notation (TASK-001 → TASK-003) for agent-generated graphs; ASCII box diagrams are copied incorrectly

section.title: Wave Analysis
section.p: Table format: Wave | Tasks | Parallel | Dependencies | Est. Tokens Wave 1: tasks with no dependencies — all can run in parallel. Wave 2: tasks depending only on Wave 1 outputs. Continue until all tasks assigned to a wave.

rule.statement: Each task entry must have: wave number, complexity (simple/normal/complex), token estimate, traces to AD-X and FR-X, depends-on list, blocks list, acceptance criteria, files table.
rule.rationale: Executor agents reading sparse task entries make up missing context. The task entry in the implementation plan is the authoritative source the TASK_NNN.md file is generated from; missing fields produce incomplete task files.

list.item: Risk table: Risk | Likelihood (Low/Med/High) | Impact (Low/Med/High) | Mitigation (concrete action)
Contingency buffer: 20% added to token estimates
Rollout: pre-deployment checklist, deployment steps, rollback plan, success criteria

rule.statement: Risk mitigations must be concrete actions, not vague hedges ("monitor for errors" vs "alert on error rate > 0.5% for 5 minutes").
rule.rationale: Vague mitigations provide no actionable guidance when the risk materializes. Concrete mitigations specify the trigger condition, the response action, and who is responsible.

text.reference: Canonical implementation-plan.md template with all sections
Topological sort validator — detect dependency cycles
Extract wave → tasks mapping from wave analysis table
